title: AutoGPT Platform graph validation fails to enforce the block 'disabled' flag, allowing disabled-block (RCE) bypass
description: AutoGPT Platform's central graph validation function _validate_graph_get_errors() in autogpt_platform/backend/backend/data/graph.py gates all graph create, update, fork, and execute paths, but its per-node loop only rejects unknown block ids and validates schema/inputs; it never checks block.disabled. The disabled flag is the access control used to keep development-only blocks such as BlockInstallationBlock (which writes attacker-supplied Python to disk and dynamically imports it) out of normal use, and it was enforced only on the direct POST /api/blocks/{id}/execute endpoint. Because validation does not honor disabled, any authenticated user can embed a disabled block as a node in a graph and have it persisted and executed, bypassing the restriction and achieving remote code execution as the backend process. The vulnerable code is the missing-authorization gap in the validation loop (lines 741-748) where a disabled-flag check should reject the block before it is accepted.
